text: Smartphone photoplethysmography (PPG) provides acceptable agreement with ECG for measuring rMSSD heart rate variability at rest.
Heart-rate chest strap sensors (Polar H7) provide acceptable agreement with ECG for measuring rMSSD heart rate variability at rest.
Both PPG and chest-strap-derived rMSSD show almost perfect correlations with ECG-derived rMSSD.
The standardized difference between consumer-device and ECG-derived rMSSD is trivial, meaning either device can be used interchangeably for HRV monitoring.
Smartphone PPG may be preferred over chest straps for athlete HRV data collection due to greater practicality and ease of use in field settings.
A 5-minute resting measurement window with guided or normal breathing is sufficient for valid rMSSD assessment using consumer devices.
